parts: Malva dendromorpha, also known as the tree mallow, is a species of flowering plant with a long history of traditional use. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its leaves, flowers, and roots, which are rich in mucilages. These mucilages, also known as demulcents, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making the plant useful for treating various ailments. The leaves and flowers of Malva dendromorpha are commonly used to make infusions and decoctions, which can be used to treat sore throats, coughs, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal parts are also rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C and potassium.

intro: Malva dendromorpha, also known as the tree mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae), characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and soft, velvety leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of Malva dendromorpha include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as mucilages and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Malva dendromorpha is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup to alleviate symptoms of coughs, sore throats, and digestive discomfort.
